checks.try: mirror those UV islands back: a flipped face samples normal maps mirrored, so lighting is wrong in a way no texture edit can fix

checks.message: 2 face(s) fall outside the 0..1 UV square
checks.try: intentional for tiling/UDIMs; a mistake for a single atlased asset - say which

checks.message: 2 face(s) stretched more than 2.0:1 (16.7% of the surface); worst 4.0:1
checks.try: add a seam through the stretched region and re-unwrap it; stretch cannot be painted out

checks.message: texel density varies 3.0x across the mesh (109.23..327.68 px/unit)
checks.try: scale island #3 up ~2.4x in the UV editor (islands are labelled in uv_layout.png); repack if it no longer fits

checks.message: the UV layout uses 45% of the square
checks.try: tighter packing = more texels per asset at the same memory; below ~50% you are paying for empty pixels

texel_density.note: texels per world unit at the given texture size; the mean is area-weighted, and spread_ratio is p98/p2 (robust to a few stray faces)

distortion.note: anisotropy = sigma_max/sigma_min of the per-face UV Jacobian: 1.0 is conformal, >2 is visibly smeared. Flipped = UV winding opposite to the mesh majority

islands.note: an island is a UV-connected face set; a seam is a 3D edge whose two faces land in different islands - so seams are counted, not guessed

packing.note: utilization is the summed UV area of all faces (1.0 = the whole square, wasted space excluded); overlap is measured on a 256x256 grid, so it finds stacked islands, not sub-texel touching
